title: QuestionEnvelope v1
description: Machine-readable schema for signed question publication on the procurement event layer.

question/id.description: Stable lifecycle identifier that links the event layer, room layer, and later procurement artifacts.

created-at.description: Publication timestamp of the question envelope.

sender/node-id.description: Swarm-facing infrastructure actor that published the envelope.

sender/participant-id.description: Participation-role identity that authored or initiated the question publication. In the MVP baseline this may be role-distinct but key-equal to the local `node-id`.

sender/federation-id.description: Federation scope of the sender when publication is federation-bound.

sender/pod-user-id.description: Hosted-user identity when publication is delegated through a later pod-backed client flow. This is additive to, not a replacement for, `sender/participant-id`.

sender/public-nym.description: Optional public-facing pseudonym shown at protocol boundaries where policy allows it.

ttl-sec.description: Time-to-live of the open request on the event layer.

question/text.description: Full textual question content published at the envelope layer in the current split architecture.

question/tags.description: Semantic tags used for routing and responder matching.

question/urgency.description: Advisory urgency classification visible to eligible responders.

delivery/scope.description: Transport-level dissemination scope derived from the chosen exposure policy.

delivery/response-channel-id.description: Room or channel identifier bound to the same question lifecycle.

follow-ups/allowed.description: Whether responders may expect iterative clarification or debate after initial publication.

request/exposure-mode.description: User-facing exposure policy chosen before submission.

room-policy/profile.description: Requested room policy profile for the answer room created from this envelope.

responder/min-reputation.description: Minimum reputation threshold for eligible responders.

reward/mode.description: Whether the request seeks open collaboration only or explicit responder procurement.

procurement/max-price-amount.description: Maximum acceptable price in minor units when procurement is enabled.

procurement/price-currency.description: Currency or settlement unit symbol associated with procurement ceiling and offers.

procurement/max-wait-sec.description: Maximum wait time acceptable to the asker when procurement is enabled.

signature.description: Detached or embedded signature container for the published envelope.

policy_annotations.description: Optional federation- or implementation-local policy annotations that do not change the core lifecycle semantics.
